newsSinceJun6: At the ADA 86th Scientific Sessions (Jun 6-8), Lilly presented positive Phase 3 TRANSCEND data for retatrutide (triple agonist; ~28% / 70 lb weight loss at the 12 mg dose over 80 weeks, simultaneously published in The Lancet) and Phase 3 Foundayo (orforglipron) results beating Novo's oral semaglutide on A1C and weight loss. Stock hit an all-time high Jun 8 (+2.16% to ~$1,149); sell-side followed with target raises (Barclays $1,400, Cantor $1,230, UBS maintained $1,250 and raised 2026 orforglipron estimates to $2B). Jun 9 flat (-0.04%), indicating the news is fully disseminated and priced.

sources.label: Motley Fool / Yahoo - Why Eli Lilly Stock Climbed to a New All-Time High (Jun 8, retatrutide ADA data)
StockStory - Why Eli Lilly (LLY) Stock Is Up Today (retatrutide Phase 3, Lancet)
GuruFocus - Eli Lilly shares rise after positive Phase 3 diabetes drug data (Foundayo/orforglipron vs oral semaglutide)
Investing.com - UBS maintains $1,250 target on orforglipron outlook

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuine new fundamental evidence (retatrutide/orforglipron Phase 3 at ADA) strengthens the durability of the human-consumption proxy, but on this alpha-weighted page LLY remains the definition of consensus: $1T+ cap, all-time high, instant sell-side target raises. New data adds quality, not hidden alpha, so rank 8 holds with modest score credit.
verdicts.bull: Retatrutide's ~28% weight loss and orforglipron's win over oral semaglutide entrench a multi-year cardiometabolic franchise duopoly position, extending the pharma-consumption cash-flow runway well past tirzepatide LOE concerns.
verdicts.neutral: Data was simultaneously published, presented at ADA, and rerated within one session (+2.2% to an all-time high); Jun 9's flat tape says the market has already cleared the news, leaving the consumption-proxy thesis intact but no fresh asymmetry.
verdicts.bear: At ~$1.03T and a fresh 52-week high with 31 analysts averaging Buy, crowding and passive ownership cap residual alpha; any retatrutide tolerability surprise, pricing/IRA pressure, or Novo competitive response hits a fully-valued name.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Retatrutide regulatory filing timeline and orforglipron (Foundayo) launch/uptake data converting ADA headlines into revenue guidance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded mega-cap positioning at all-time highs: GLP-1 pricing pressure, supply/competition from Novo, or trial safety signals would de-rate a consensus-owned name with little alpha cushion.
